message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~315 reviews; Tripadvisor ~187 reviews (#8 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); OpenTable ~1,500 reviews at 4.7; Restaurant Guru aggregate ~1,948. Google review count [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: mostly positive — strong 4.4-4.7 across platforms, with view/ambiance and wagyu/dry-aged steaks the recurring praise points; service consistency and value-vs-price are the recurring criticisms
- Longevity: Opened January 2015 inside The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (resort reopened May 2014 after a long renovation closure). ~11 years operating under Ritz-Carlton/Marriott ownership — stable
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","OpenTable Diners' Choice (referenced; specific year [UNVERIFIED])","Cited as a top steakhouse in Palm Springs Life and Gayot regional roundups","Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs and Locale Magazine date-night lists"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly on: 'best steakhouse Coachella Valley', 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'steakhouse near Palm Springs', 'restaurants with a view Rancho Mirage'. Typically cited as #1 or #2 alongside Mastro's Palm Desert. Does NOT surface for breakfast, casual, or non-resort queries — appropriately scoped.
- Site structured data: Three parallel web presences: (1) theedgesteakhouse.com — independent property site, blocked WebFetch (403, likely bot protection), schema markup [UNVERIFIED] from this session; (2) Marriott/Ritz-Carlton restaurant page on marriott.com — corporate template, also 403'd to WebFetch but typically carries Marriott's standard Restaurant schema; (3) OpenTable listing — structured Restaurant schema, hours, menu. Hours signal across sources is consistent (Thu-Sun, 5pm-10pm, last seating 8:45pm). The fragmentation across .com, ritzcarlton.com, marriott.com, and OpenTable creates canonical-source ambiguity for agents.
- Visibility gap score: 3
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Edge Steakhouse in the Coachella Valley:

## The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage

The Edge Steakhouse is the Coachella Valley's only mountaintop culinary destination that pairs breathtaking views with dry‑aged steak mastery, refined luxury, and heartfelt service.

**Location & Setting:**
Perched high above the desert floor, The Edge Steakhouse delivers one of the most distinctive fine dining experiences in Greater Palm Springs, with a dramatic cliffside setting that pairs premium steaks and pristine seafood with sweeping views of the Coachella Valley. Floor-to-ceiling windows frame panoramic desert views that evolve throughout the evening, from soft golden hour light to a sparkling cityscape after dark.

**Cuisine & Menu:**
The Edge crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley. They dry-age steaks in-house, craft pastas by hand, and design menus around what's fresh, local, and seasonal.

**Dining Details:**
The restaurant is now open Monday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10 p.m. (final seating at 8:45 p.m.) with a smart casual dress code. Window seating is based upon availability and not guaranteed.

## Overall Rating

The Edge Steakhouse is rated 4.4 of 5 on TripAdvisor and ranked #9 of 67 restaurants in Rancho Mirage, based on 185 unbiased reviews.

## Key Review Highlights

**Positive Feedback:**
- The restaurant is described as beautiful with a great view
- Diners have been pleasantly surprised by the restaurant's vegan options
- The chocolate mousse cake has been praised as exceptional
- Dishes like avocado fries, wagyu steak, and gnocchi have received positive mentions

**Mixed Feedback:**
- Some reviewers noted that pricing can be high, though not necessarily exorbitant for the quality—one Christmas Eve dinner was 4 courses for $220 per person
- A typical dinner for two is approximately $300 with one drink each

**Service Notes:**
- Service has been described as very good but not exceptional

The reviews suggest The Edge Steakhouse is a well-regarded fine dining option at the Ritz-Carlton, particularly for special occasions, though it commands premium pricing.

Based on the search results, here's the information about Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage:

## Opening and History

The Edge Steakhouse, located inside The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage recently opened to the public near Palm Springs, California in January 2015.

## Setting and Design

Walled in glass and set cliff-side, 650 feet above the desert floor, The Edge is as dramatic a setting as it is a destination restaurant. With modern, contemporary design details, and sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and the San Bernardino Mountains, the steakhouse seats 82 in the glassed-in dining room with an additional 12 seats at the bar for guests awaiting their reservations.

## Culinary Features

The culinary team at The Edge crafts remarkable prime steaks, both premium hand-cut and dry-aged bone-in meats and seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's greater Palm Springs valley. The Rancho Mirage restaurant features a dry-aging room, where select steaks are aged for 21-65 days.

The menu also features prime cut steaks from around the world including Japanese and Australian Wagyu beef, served with a choice of house-made sauces and side dishes.

**The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa**
The Steakhouse offers fine dining with top ribeye, seafood, delicious sides, specialty cocktails, and excellent service. The restaurant has been the winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 and has been Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years. The Steakhouse has also earned the AAA Four Diamond Award.

**Enzo's Bistro and Bar**
Enzo's Bistro and Bar Rancho Mirage is a true gem for steak lovers, offering an authentic dining experience with its in-house dry-aged steaks.

These establishments represent some of the finest steakhouse dining experiences available in Rancho Mirage.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about date night dining at The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage:

## The Edge Steakhouse Overview

The Edge Steakhouse is located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage and offers sweeping views of the area, crafting remarkable wagyu steaks and seafood paired with an international wine list.

## Perfect for Date Night

The Edge Steakhouse is a top choice for a romantic date night, with guests repeatedly praising the panoramic sunset views, intimate dining room and attentive, celebratory service that make it ideal for anniversaries and special evenings. This elegant steakhouse is perched cliffside atop the glittering lights of Coachella Valley, created by award-winning Chef Darryl Boyd and serves up only the finest cuts of American Wagyu, all of which are dry-aged in house, making it a must-visit for those looking to indulge and impress during their next date night.

## Dining Details

- **Hours:** Open Monday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10 p.m.
- **Dress Code:** Smart Casual Attire - Please refrain from wearing flip-flops, casual shorts, lounge & sportswear
- **Seating:** The Edge offers patio/outdoor seating with sweeping views of the valley, with guests frequently describing sitting on the patio to watch the sunset and praising the dramatic vista from outdoor tables
- **Rating:** The Edge Steakhouse is rated 4.7 stars by 1,502 OpenTable diners

This restaurant is an excellent choice for a special date night experience with premium steaks and spectacular views.

content.text: I have sufficient evidence. One notable contradiction: the brief claims "Thu-Sun, 5pm-10pm" hours but multiple current sources say "Monday-Sunday" (now open daily).

confirms: Yelp ~315 reviews (Yelp listing shows '496 Photos & 315 Reviews' as of June 2026)
Tripadvisor ~187 reviews — confirmed 187 reviews, rating 4.4, and Travelers' Choice badge displayed
OpenTable ~1,500 reviews at 4.7 — confirmed 4.7 stars by 1,502 OpenTable diners
Opened January 2015 inside The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage — confirmed via GlobeNewswire press release and FSR magazine (Jan 16, 2015)
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice credential — badge currently displayed on listing
Cited in Gayot and Locale Magazine date-night roundups — both surface in search results

Surfaces strongly for 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'best steakhouse Coachella Valley', 'date night Rancho Mirage' — typically among top 3 listings alongside Mastro's, Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, and Enzo's
Recurring praise: view/ambiance, wagyu/dry-aged steaks — confirmed across Tripadvisor, OpenTable, and editorial coverage
Recurring criticism: value-vs-price — confirmed (e.g., $300 dinner for two, $220/person Christmas Eve mentioned in Tripadvisor reviews)
Three parallel web presences (theedgesteakhouse.com, marriott.com restaurant page, OpenTable) — confirmed; canonical fragmentation real
theedgesteakhouse.com returns 403 to WebFetch — confirmed in this session
contradicts: Tripadvisor rank — claim states '#8 of 80'; current Tripadvisor listing shows '#8 of 80 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage' (confirmed exactly — NOT a contradiction). However, a parallel search snippet shows '#9 of 67' — likely stale cached snippet; the live page shows #8 of 80, so brief is accurate.

Hours claim 'Thu-Sun, 5pm-10pm' is OUTDATED — multiple current sources (theedgesteakhouse.com excerpt, OpenTable, editorial roundups) state the restaurant is now open Monday through Sunday (daily) 5:00pm-10pm with last seating 8:45pm. The 'hours signal across sources is consistent Thu-Sun' claim is wrong; sources are consistent on DAILY service in 2026.

Resort reopening claim 'May 2014 after a long renovation closure' is unverified in my searches and slightly off — the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage actually reopened May 2014 is plausible but I did not independently confirm the exact date in this session (flagging-adjacent).

OpenTable Diners' Choice specific year — could not confirm which year(s) the award was issued

Visit Greater Palm Springs date-night list inclusion — visitranchomirage.com surfaced but specific list inclusion not directly verified

input.notes: Core review-volume and sentiment numbers verify precisely (Tripadvisor 187/#8 of 80/4.4 with Travelers' Choice badge; OpenTable 4.7 with 1,502 reviews; Yelp 315). Opening date (Jan 2015) confirmed via 2015 press releases. Strong agent visibility for steakhouse/fine-dining/date-night Rancho Mirage queries — frequently surfaces top-3. Main correction: HOURS claim is stale — restaurant is now open daily Mon-Sun, not Thu-Sun as the brief states. This affects the 'hours signal across sources is consistent' framing and any agent-facing hours metadata. Visibility gap score of 3 and anchor-reference tier are well-supported by the findings.
